candidate_problems.text: Can there be cases where multiple continuity criteria are equally legitimate yet yield incompatible verdicts with no higher-order rule for choosing among them?
What determines which invariants matter in a given explanatory context: practical purpose, institutional convention, causal structure, or something else?
Are there identity questions in science or law where a context-free fact of the matter does exist, and what structurally distinguishes them from Theseus cases?
